<pr_description>
`vello_cpu` crashes if you keep painting after calling `flush` and not resetting
Example:

```rs
ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, WIDTH as f64, HEIGHT as f64));
ctx.flush();
ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, WIDTH as f64, HEIGHT as f64));
ctx.flush();
ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, WIDTH as f64, HEIGHT as f64));
```

</pr_description>

     1	// Copyright 2025 the Vello Authors
     2	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 OR MIT
     3	
     4	//! Vello CPU is a 2D graphics rendering engine written in Rust, for devices with no or underpowered GPUs.
     5	//!
     6	//! It is currently available as an alpha.
     7	//! See the [Caveats](#caveats) section for things you need to be aware of.
     8	//!
     9	//! We also develop [Vello](https://crates.io/crates/vello), which makes use of the GPU for 2D rendering and has higher performance than Vello CPU.
    10	//! Vello CPU is being developed as part of work to address shortcomings in Vello.
    11	//!
    12	//! ## Usage
    13	//!
    14	//! To use Vello CPU, you need to:
    15	//!
    16	//! - Create a [`RenderContext`][], a 2D drawing context for a fixed-size target area.
    17	//! - For each object in your scene:
    18	//!   - Set how the object will be painted, using [`set_paint`][RenderContext::set_paint].
    19	//!   - Set the shape to be drawn for that object, using methods like [`fill_path`][RenderContext::fill_path],
    20	//!     [`stroke_path`][RenderContext::stroke_path], or [`glyph_run`][RenderContext::glyph_run].
    21	//! - Render it to an image using [`RenderContext::render_to_pixmap`][].
    22	//!
    23	//! ```rust
    24	//! use vello_cpu::{RenderContext, Pixmap, RenderMode};
    25	//! use vello_cpu::{color::{palette::css, PremulRgba8}, kurbo::Rect};
    26	//! let width = 10;
    27	//! let height = 5;
    28	//! let mut context = RenderContext::new(width, height);
    29	//! context.set_paint(css::MAGENTA);
    30	//! context.fill_rect(&Rect::from_points((3., 1.), (7., 4.)));
    31	//!
    32	//! let mut target = Pixmap::new(width, height);
    33	//! // This is only necessary if you activated the `multithreading` feature.
    34	//! context.flush();
    35	//! context.render_to_pixmap(&mut target, RenderMode::default());
    36	//!
    37	//! let expected_render = b"\
    38	//!     0000000000\
    39	//!     0001111000\
    40	//!     0001111000\
    41	//!     0001111000\
    42	//!     0000000000";
    43	//! let magenta = css::MAGENTA.premultiply().to_rgba8();
    44	//! let transparent = PremulRgba8 {r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, a: 0};
    45	//! let mut result = Vec::new();
    46	//! for pixel in target.data() {
    47	//!     if *pixel == magenta {
    48	//!         result.push(b'1');
    49	//!     } else if *pixel == transparent {
    50	//!         result.push(b'0');
    51	//!     } else {
    52	//!          panic!("Got unexpected pixel value {pixel:?}");
    53	//!     }
    54	//! }
    55	//! assert_eq!(&result, expected_render);
    56	//! ```
    57	//!
    58	//! ## Features
    59	//!
    60	//! - `std` (enabled by default): Get floating point functions from the standard library
    61	//!   (likely using your target's libc).
    62	//! - `libm`: Use floating point implementations from `libm`.
    63	//! - `png`(enabled by default): Allow loading [`Pixmap`]s from PNG images.
    64	//!   Also required for rendering glyphs with an embedded PNG.
    65	//! - `multithreading`: Enable multi-threaded rendering.
    66	//!
    67	//! At least one of `std` and `libm` is required; `std` overrides `libm`.
    68	//!
    69	//! ## Caveats
    70	//!
    71	//! Vello CPU is an alpha for several reasons, including the following.
    72	//!
    73	//! ### API stability
    74	//!
    75	//! This API has been developed for an initial version, and has no stability guarantees.
    76	//! Whilst we are in the `0.0.x` release series, any release is likely to breaking.
    77	//! We have known plans to change the API around how image resources are used.
    78	//!
    79	//! ### Documentation
    80	//!
    81	//! We have not yet put any work into documentation.
    82	//!
    83	//! ### Performance
    84	//!
    85	//! We do not perform several important optimisations, such as the use of multithreading and SIMD.
    86	//! Additionally, some algorithms we use aren't final, and will be replaced with higher-performance variants.
    87	//!
    88	//! ## Implementation
    89	//!
    90	//! TODO: Point to documentation of sparse strips pattern.
    91	// LINEBENDER LINT SET - lib.rs - v3
    92	// See https://linebender.org/wiki/canonical-lints/
    93	// These lints shouldn't apply to examples or tests.
    94	#![cfg_attr(not(test), warn(unused_crate_dependencies))]
    95	// These lints shouldn't apply to examples.
    96	#![warn(clippy::print_stdout, clippy::print_stderr)]
    97	// Targeting e.g. 32-bit means structs containing usize can give false positives for 64-bit.
    98	#![cfg_attr(target_pointer_width = "64", warn(clippy::trivially_copy_pass_by_ref))]
    99	// END LINEBENDER LINT SET
   100	#![cfg_attr(docsrs, feature(doc_auto_cfg))]
   101	#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
   102	#![expect(
   103	    clippy::cast_possible_truncation,
   104	    reason = "We cast u16s to u8 in various places where we know for sure that it's < 256"
   105	)]
   106	#![no_std]
   107	
   108	extern crate alloc;
   109	extern crate core;
   110	#[cfg(feature = "std")]
   111	extern crate std;
   112	
   113	mod render;
   114

   116	#[doc(hidden)]

   131	
   132	/// The selected rendering mode.
   133	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Default)]
   134	pub enum RenderMode {
   135	    /// Optimize speed (by performing calculations with u8/16).
   136	    #[default]
   137	    OptimizeSpeed,
   138	    /// Optimize quality (by performing calculations with f32).
   139	    OptimizeQuality,
   140	}

     4	//! Basic render operations.
     5	
     6	use crate::RenderMode;
     7	use crate::dispatch::Dispatcher;
     8	#[cfg(feature = "multithreading")]
     9	use crate::dispatch::multi_threaded::MultiThreadedDispatcher;
    10	use crate::dispatch::single_threaded::SingleThreadedDispatcher;

    35	
    36	pub(crate) const DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 0.1;
    37	/// A render context.
    38	#[derive(Debug)]
    39	pub struct RenderContext {
    40	    pub(crate) width: u16,
    41	    pub(crate) height: u16,
    42	    pub(crate) paint: PaintType,
    43	    pub(crate) paint_transform: Affine,
    44	    pub(crate) stroke: Stroke,
    45	    pub(crate) transform: Affine,
    46	    pub(crate) fill_rule: Fill,
    47	    pub(crate) temp_path: BezPath,
    48	    pub(crate) encoded_paints: Vec<EncodedPaint>,
    49	    #[cfg_attr(
    50	        not(feature = "text"),
    51	        allow(dead_code, reason = "used when the `text` feature is enabled")
    52	    )]
    53	    pub(crate) level: Level,
    54	    dispatcher: Box<dyn Dispatcher>,
    55	}
    56	
    57	/// Settings to apply to the render context.
    58	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]
    59	pub struct RenderSettings {
    60	    /// The SIMD level that should be used for rendering operations.
    61	    pub level: Level,
    62	    /// The number of worker threads that should be used for rendering. Only has an effect
    63	    /// if the `multithreading` feature is active.
    64	    pub num_threads: u16,
    65	}
    66	
    67	impl Default for RenderSettings {
    68	    fn default() -> Self {
    69	        Self {
    70	            level: Level::new(),
    71	            #[cfg(feature = "multithreading")]
    72	            num_threads: std::thread::available_parallelism()
    73	                .unwrap()
    74	                .get()
    75	                .saturating_sub(1) as u16,
    76	            #[cfg(not(feature = "multithreading"))]
    77	            num_threads: 0,
    78	        }
    79	    }
    80	}
    81	
    82	impl RenderContext {
    83	    /// Create a new render context with the given width and height in pixels.
    84	    pub fn new(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self {
    85	        let settings = RenderSettings::default();
    86	        Self::new_inner(width, height, settings.num_threads, settings.level)
    87	    }
    88	
    89	    /// Create a new render context with specific settings.
    90	    pub fn new_with(width: u16, height: u16, settings: &RenderSettings) -> Self {
    91	        Self::new_inner(width, height, settings.num_threads, settings.level)
    92	    }
    93	
    94	    fn new_inner(width: u16, height: u16, num_threads: u16, level: Level) -> Self {
    95	        #[cfg(feature = "multithreading")]
    96	        let dispatcher: Box<dyn Dispatcher> = if num_threads == 0 {
    97	            Box::new(SingleThreadedDispatcher::new(width, height, level))
    98	        } else {
    99	            Box::new(MultiThreadedDispatcher::new(
   100	                width,
   101	                height,
   102	                num_threads,
   103	                level,
   104	            ))
   105	        };
   106	
   107	        #[cfg(not(feature = "multithreading"))]
   108	        let dispatcher: Box<dyn Dispatcher> = {
   109	            let _ = num_threads;
   110	            Box::new(SingleThreadedDispatcher::new(width, height, level))
   111	        };
   112	
   113	        let transform = Affine::IDENTITY;
   114	        let fill_rule = Fill::NonZero;
   115	        let paint = BLACK.into();
   116	        let paint_transform = Affine::IDENTITY;
   117	        let stroke = Stroke {
   118	            width: 1.0,
   119	            join: Join::Bevel,
   120	            start_cap: Cap::Butt,
   121	            end_cap: Cap::Butt,
   122	            ..Default::default()
   123	        };
   124	        let encoded_paints = vec![];
   125	        let temp_path = BezPath::new();
   126	
   127	        Self {
   128	            width,
   129	            height,
   130	            dispatcher,
   131	            transform,
   132	            paint,
   133	            paint_transform,
   134	            fill_rule,
   135	            level,
   136	            stroke,
   137	            temp_path,
   138	            encoded_paints,
   139	        }
   140	    }
   141	
   142	    fn encode_current_paint(&mut self) -> Paint {
   143	        match self.paint.clone() {
   144	            PaintType::Solid(s) => s.into(),
   145	            PaintType::Gradient(g) => {
   146	                // TODO: Add caching?
   147	                g.encode_into(
   148	                    &mut self.encoded_paints,
   149	                    self.transform * self.paint_transform,
   150	                )
   151	            }
   152	            PaintType::Image(i) => i.encode_into(
   153	                &mut self.encoded_paints,
   154	                self.transform * self.paint_transform,
   155	            ),
   156	        }
   157	    }
   158	
   159	    /// Fill a path.
   160	    pub fn fill_path(&mut self, path: &BezPath) {
   161	        let paint = self.encode_current_paint();
   162	        self.dispatcher
   163	            .fill_path(path, self.fill_rule, self.transform, paint);
   164	    }
   165	
   166	    /// Stroke a path.
   167	    pub fn stroke_path(&mut self, path: &BezPath) {
   168	        let paint = self.encode_current_paint();
   169	        self.dispatcher
   170	            .stroke_path(path, &self.stroke, self.transform, paint);
   171	    }
   172	
   173	    /// Fill a rectangle.
   174	    pub fn fill_rect(&mut self, rect: &Rect) {
   175	        // Don't use `rect.to_path` here, because it will perform a new allocation, which
   176	        // profiling showed can become a bottleneck for many small rectangles.
   177	        // TODO: Generalize this so that for example `blurred_rectangle` and other places
   178	        // can also profit from this.
   179	        self.temp_path.truncate(0);
   180	        self.temp_path
   181	            .push(PathEl::MoveTo(Point::new(rect.x0, rect.y0)));
   182	        self.temp_path
   183	            .push(PathEl::LineTo(Point::new(rect.x1, rect.y0)));
   184	        self.temp_path
   185	            .push(PathEl::LineTo(Point::new(rect.x1, rect.y1)));
   186	        self.temp_path
   187	            .push(PathEl::LineTo(Point::new(rect.x0, rect.y1)));
   188	        self.temp_path.push(PathEl::ClosePath);
   189	
   190	        let paint = self.encode_current_paint();
   191	        self.dispatcher
   192	            .fill_path(&self.temp_path, self.fill_rule, self.transform, paint);
   193	    }
   194	
   195	    /// Fill a blurred rectangle with the given radius and standard deviation.
   196	    ///
   197	    /// Note that this only works properly if the current paint is set to a solid color.
   198	    /// If not, it will fall back to using black as the fill color.
   199	    pub fn fill_blurred_rounded_rect(&mut self, rect: &Rect, radius: f32, std_dev: f32) {
   200	        let color = match self.paint {
   201	            PaintType::Solid(s) => s,
   202	            // Fallback to black when attempting to blur a rectangle with an image/gradient paint
   203	            _ => BLACK,
   204	        };
   205	
   206	        let blurred_rect = BlurredRoundedRectangle {
   207	            rect: *rect,
   208	            color,
   209	            radius,
   210	            std_dev,
   211	        };
   212	
   213	        // The actual rectangle we paint needs to be larger so that the blurring effect
   214	        // is not cut off.
   215	        // The impulse response of a gaussian filter is infinite.
   216	        // For performance reason we cut off the filter at some extent where the response is close to zero.
   217	        let kernel_size = 2.5 * std_dev;
   218	        let inflated_rect = rect.inflate(f64::from(kernel_size), f64::from(kernel_size));
   219	        let transform = self.transform * self.paint_transform;
   220	
   221	        let paint = blurred_rect.encode_into(&mut self.encoded_paints, transform);
   222	        self.dispatcher.fill_path(
   223	            &inflated_rect.to_path(0.1),
   224	            Fill::NonZero,
   225	            self.transform,
   226	            paint,
   227	        );
   228	    }
   229	
   230	    /// Stroke a rectangle.
   231	    pub fn stroke_rect(&mut self, rect: &Rect) {
   232	        self.stroke_path(&rect.to_path(DEFAULT_TOLERANCE));
   233	    }
   234	
   235	    /// Creates a builder for drawing a run of glyphs that have the same attributes.
   236	    #[cfg(feature = "text")]
   237	    pub fn glyph_run(&mut self, font: &crate::peniko::Font) -> GlyphRunBuilder<'_, Self> {
   238	        GlyphRunBuilder::new(font.clone(), self.transform, self)
   239	    }
   240	
   241	    /// Push a new layer with the given properties.
   242	    ///
   243	    /// Note that the mask, if provided, needs to have the same size as the render context. Otherwise,
   244	    /// it will be ignored. In addition to that, the mask will not be affected by the current
   245	    /// transformation matrix in place.
   246	    pub fn push_layer(
   247	        &mut self,
   248	        clip_path: Option<&BezPath>,
   249	        blend_mode: Option<BlendMode>,
   250	        opacity: Option<f32>,
   251	        mask: Option<Mask>,
   252	    ) {
   253	        let mask = mask.and_then(|m| {
   254	            if m.width() != self.width || m.height() != self.height {
   255	                None
   256	            } else {
   257	                Some(m)
   258	            }
   259	        });
   260	
   261	        let blend_mode = blend_mode.unwrap_or(BlendMode::new(Mix::Normal, Compose::SrcOver));
   262	        let opacity = opacity.unwrap_or(1.0);
   263	
   264	        self.dispatcher.push_layer(
   265	            clip_path,
   266	            self.fill_rule,
   267	            self.transform,
   268	            blend_mode,
   269	            opacity,
   270	            mask,
   271	        );
   272	    }
   273	
   274	    /// Push a new clip layer.
   275	    pub fn push_clip_layer(&mut self, path: &BezPath) {
   276	        self.push_layer(Some(path), None, None, None);
   277	    }
   278	
   279	    /// Push a new blend layer.
   280	    pub fn push_blend_layer(&mut self, blend_mode: BlendMode) {
   281	        self.push_layer(None, Some(blend_mode), None, None);
   282	    }
   283	
   284	    /// Push a new opacity layer.
   285	    pub fn push_opacity_layer(&mut self, opacity: f32) {
   286	        self.push_layer(None, None, Some(opacity), None);
   287	    }
   288	
   289	    /// Push a new mask layer.
   290	    ///
   291	    /// Note that the mask, if provided, needs to have the same size as the render context. Otherwise,
   292	    /// it will be ignored. In addition to that, the mask will not be affected by the current
   293	    /// transformation matrix in place.
   294	    pub fn push_mask_layer(&mut self, mask: Mask) {
   295	        self.push_layer(None, None, None, Some(mask));
   296	    }
   297	
   298	    /// Pop the last-pushed layer.
   299	    pub fn pop_layer(&mut self) {
   300	        self.dispatcher.pop_layer();
   301	    }
   302	
   303	    /// Set the current stroke.
   304	    pub fn set_stroke(&mut self, stroke: Stroke) {
   305	        self.stroke = stroke;
   306	    }
   307	
   308	    /// Get the current stroke
   309	    pub fn stroke(&self) -> &Stroke {
   310	        &self.stroke
   311	    }
   312	
   313	    /// Set the current paint.
   314	    pub fn set_paint(&mut self, paint: impl Into<PaintType>) {
   315	        self.paint = paint.into();
   316	    }
   317	
   318	    /// Get the current paint.
   319	    pub fn paint(&self) -> &PaintType {
   320	        &self.paint
   321	    }
   322	
   323	    /// Set the current paint transform.
   324	    ///
   325	    /// The paint transform is applied to the paint after the transform of the geometry the paint
   326	    /// is drawn in, i.e., the paint transform is applied after the global transform. This allows
   327	    /// transforming the paint independently from the drawn geometry.
   328	    pub fn set_paint_transform(&mut self, paint_transform: Affine) {
   329	        self.paint_transform = paint_transform;
   330	    }
   331	
   332	    /// Get the current paint transform.
   333	    pub fn paint_transform(&self) -> &Affine {
   334	        &self.paint_transform
   335	    }
   336	
   337	    /// Reset the current paint transform.
   338	    pub fn reset_paint_transform(&mut self) {
   339	        self.paint_transform = Affine::IDENTITY;
   340	    }
   341	
   342	    /// Set the current fill rule.
   343	    pub fn set_fill_rule(&mut self, fill_rule: Fill) {
   344	        self.fill_rule = fill_rule;
   345	    }
   346	
   347	    /// Get the current fill rule.
   348	    pub fn fill_rule(&self) -> &Fill {
   349	        &self.fill_rule
   350	    }
   351	
   352	    /// Set the current transform.
   353	    pub fn set_transform(&mut self, transform: Affine) {
   354	        self.transform = transform;
   355	    }
   356	
   357	    /// Get the current transform.
   358	    pub fn transform(&self) -> &Affine {
   359	        &self.transform
   360	    }
   361	
   362	    /// Reset the current transform.
   363	    pub fn reset_transform(&mut self) {
   364	        self.transform = Affine::IDENTITY;
   365	    }
   366	
   367	    /// Reset the render context.
   368	    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
   369	        self.dispatcher.reset();
   370	        self.encoded_paints.clear();
   371	        self.reset_transform();
   372	        self.reset_paint_transform();
   373	    }
   374	
   375	    /// Flush any pending operations.
   376	    ///
   377	    /// This is a no-op when using the single-threaded render mode, and can be ignored.
   378	    /// For multi-threaded rendering, you _have_ to call this before rasterizing, otherwise
   379	    /// the program will panic.
   380	    pub fn flush(&mut self) {
   381	        self.dispatcher.flush();
   382	    }
   383	
   384	    /// Render the current context into a buffer.
   385	    /// The buffer is expected to be in premultiplied RGBA8 format with length `width * height * 4`
   386	    pub fn render_to_buffer(
   387	        &self,
   388	        buffer: &mut [u8],
   389	        width: u16,
   390	        height: u16,
   391	        render_mode: RenderMode,
   392	    ) {
   393	        // TODO: Maybe we should move those checks into the dispatcher.
   394	        let wide = self.dispatcher.wide();
   395	        assert!(!wide.has_layers(), "some layers haven't been popped yet");
   396	        assert_eq!(
   397	            buffer.len(),
   398	            (width as usize) * (height as usize) * 4,
   399	            "provided width ({}) and height ({}) do not match buffer size ({})",
   400	            width,
   401	            height,
   402	            buffer.len(),
   403	        );
   404	
   405	        self.dispatcher
   406	            .rasterize(buffer, render_mode, width, height, &self.encoded_paints);
   407	    }
   408	
   409	    /// Render the current context into a pixmap.
   410	    pub fn render_to_pixmap(&self, pixmap: &mut Pixmap, render_mode: RenderMode) {
   411	        let width = pixmap.width();
   412	        let height = pixmap.height();
   413	        self.render_to_buffer(pixmap.data_as_u8_slice_mut(), width, height, render_mode);
   414	    }
   415	
   416	    /// Return the width of the pixmap.
   417	    pub fn width(&self) -> u16 {
   418	        self.width
   419	    }
   420	
   421	    /// Return the height of the pixmap.
   422	    pub fn height(&self) -> u16 {
   423	        self.height
   424	    }
   425	}
   426	
   427	#[cfg(feature = "text")]
   428	impl GlyphRenderer for RenderContext {
   429	    fn fill_glyph(&mut self, prepared_glyph: PreparedGlyph<'_>) {
   430	        match prepared_glyph.glyph_type {
   431	            GlyphType::Outline(glyph) => {
   432	                let paint = self.encode_current_paint();
   433	                self.dispatcher.fill_path(
   434	                    glyph.path,
   435	                    Fill::NonZero,
   436	                    prepared_glyph.transform,
   437	                    paint,
   438	                );
   439	            }
   440	            GlyphType::Bitmap(glyph) => {
   441	                // We need to change the state of the render context
   442	                // to render the bitmap, but don't want to pollute the context,
   443	                // so simulate a `save` and `restore` operation.
   444	                let old_transform = self.transform;
   445	                let old_paint = self.paint.clone();
   446	
   447	                // If we scale down by a large factor, fall back to cubic scaling.
   448	                let quality = if prepared_glyph.transform.as_coeffs()[0] < 0.5
   449	                    || prepared_glyph.transform.as_coeffs()[3] < 0.5
   450	                {
   451	                    crate::peniko::ImageQuality::High
   452	                } else {
   453	                    crate::peniko::ImageQuality::Medium
   454	                };
   455	
   456	                let image = vello_common::paint::Image {
   457	                    source: ImageSource::Pixmap(Arc::new(glyph.pixmap)),
   458	                    x_extend: crate::peniko::Extend::Pad,
   459	                    y_extend: crate::peniko::Extend::Pad,
   460	                    quality,
   461	                };
   462	
   463	                self.set_paint(image);
   464	                self.set_transform(prepared_glyph.transform);
   465	                self.fill_rect(&glyph.area);
   466	
   467	                // Restore the state.
   468	                self.set_paint(old_paint);
   469	                self.transform = old_transform;
   470	            }
   471	            GlyphType::Colr(glyph) => {
   472	                // Same as for bitmap glyphs, save the state and restore it later on.
   473	                let old_transform = self.transform;
   474	               <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

     1	// Copyright 2025 the Vello Authors
     2	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 OR MIT
     3	
     4	use crate::RenderMode;
     5	use crate::dispatch::Dispatcher;
     6	use crate::fine::{F32Kernel, Fine, FineKernel, U8Kernel};
     7	use crate::kurbo::{Affine, BezPath, Stroke};
     8	use crate::peniko::{BlendMode, Fill};
     9	use crate::region::Regions;
    10	use crate::strip_generator::StripGenerator;
    11	use vello_common::coarse::Wide;
    12	use vello_common::encode::EncodedPaint;
    13	use vello_common::fearless_simd::{Fallback, Level, Simd};
    14	use vello_common::mask::Mask;
    15	use vello_common::paint::Paint;
    16	
    17	#[derive(Debug)]
    18	pub(crate) struct SingleThreadedDispatcher {
    19	    wide: Wide,
    20	    strip_generator: StripGenerator,
    21	    level: Level,
    22	}
    23	
    24	impl SingleThreadedDispatcher {
    25	    pub(crate) fn new(width: u16, height: u16, level: Level) -> Self {
    26	        let wide = Wide::new(width, height);
    27	        let strip_generator = StripGenerator::new(width, height, level);
    28	
    29	        Self {
    30	            wide,
    31	            strip_generator,
    32	            level,
    33	        }
    34	    }
    35	
    36	    fn rasterize_with<S: Simd, F: FineKernel<S>>(
    37	        &self,
    38	        simd: S,
    39	        buffer: &mut [u8],
    40	        width: u16,
    41	        height: u16,
    42	        encoded_paints: &[EncodedPaint],
    43	    ) {
    44	        let mut buffer = Regions::new(width, height, buffer);
    45	        let mut fine = Fine::<S, F>::new(simd);
    46	
    47	        buffer.update_regions(|region| {
    48	            let x = region.x;
    49	            let y = region.y;
    50	
    51	            let wtile = self.wide.get(x, y);
    52	            fine.set_coords(x, y);
    53	
    54	            fine.clear(wtile.bg);
    55	            for cmd in &wtile.cmds {
    56	                fine.run_cmd(cmd, self.strip_generator.alpha_buf(), encoded_paints);
    57	            }
    58	
    59	            fine.pack(region);
    60	        });
    61	    }
    62	}
    63	
    64	impl Dispatcher for SingleThreadedDispatcher {
    65	    fn wide(&self) -> &Wide {
    66	        &self.wide
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    fn fill_path(&mut self, path: &BezPath, fill_rule: Fill, transform: Affine, paint: Paint) {
    70	        let wide = &mut self.wide;
    71	
    72	        let func = |strips| wide.generate(strips, fill_rule, paint, 0);
    73	        self.strip_generator
    74	            .generate_filled_path(path, fill_rule, transform, func);
    75	    }
    76	
    77	    fn stroke_path(&mut self, path: &BezPath, stroke: &Stroke, transform: Affine, paint: Paint) {
    78	        let wide = &mut self.wide;
    79	
    80	        let func = |strips| wide.generate(strips, Fill::NonZero, paint, 0);
    81	        self.strip_generator
    82	            .generate_stroked_path(path, stroke, transform, func);
    83	    }
    84	
    85	    fn push_layer(
    86	        &mut self,
    87	        clip_path: Option<&BezPath>,
    88	        fill_rule: Fill,
    89	        clip_transform: Affine,
    90	        blend_mode: BlendMode,
    91	        opacity: f32,
    92	        mask: Option<Mask>,
    93	    ) {
    94	        let clip = if let Some(c) = clip_path {
    95	            // This variable will always be assigned to in the closure, but the compiler can't recognize that.
    96	            // So just assign a dummy value here.
    97	            let mut strip_buf = &[][..];
    98	
    99	            self.strip_generator
   100	                .generate_filled_path(c, fill_rule, clip_transform, |strips| strip_buf = strips);
   101	
   102	            Some((strip_buf, fill_rule))
   103	        } else {
   104	            None
   105	        };
   106	
   107	        self.wide.push_layer(clip, blend_mode, mask, opacity, 0);
   108	    }
   109	
   110	    fn pop_layer(&mut self) {
   111	        self.wide.pop_layer();
   112	    }
   113	
   114	    fn reset(&mut self) {
   115	        self.wide.reset();
   116	        self.strip_generator.reset();
   117	    }
   118	
   119	    fn flush(&mut self) {}
   120	
   121	    fn rasterize(
   122	        &self,
   123	        buffer: &mut [u8],
   124	        render_mode: RenderMode,
   125	        width: u16,
   126	        height: u16,
   127	        encoded_paints: &[EncodedPaint],
   128	    ) {
   129	        match render_mode {
   130	            RenderMode::OptimizeSpeed => match self.level {
   131	                #[cfg(all(feature = "std", target_arch = "aarch64"))]
   132	                Level::Neon(n) => {
   133	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::Neon, U8Kernel>(
   134	                        n,
   135	                        buffer,
   136	                        width,
   137	                        height,
   138	                        encoded_paints,
   139	                    );
   140	                }
   141	                #[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", target_feature = "simd128"))]
   142	                Level::WasmSimd128(w) => {
   143	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::WasmSimd128, U8Kernel>(
   144	                        w,
   145	                        buffer,
   146	                        width,
   147	                        height,
   148	                        encoded_paints,
   149	                    );
   150	                }
   151	                _ => self.rasterize_with::<Fallback, U8Kernel>(
   152	                    Fallback::new(),
   153	                    buffer,
   154	                    width,
   155	                    height,
   156	                    encoded_paints,
   157	                ),
   158	            },
   159	            RenderMode::OptimizeQuality => match self.level {
   160	                #[cfg(all(feature = "std", target_arch = "aarch64"))]
   161	                Level::Neon(n) => {
   162	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::Neon, F32Kernel>(
   163	                        n,
   164	                        buffer,
   165	                        width,
   166	                        height,
   167	                        encoded_paints,
   168	                    );
   169	                }
   170	                #[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", target_feature = "simd128"))]
   171	                Level::WasmSimd128(w) => {
   172	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::WasmSimd128, F32Kernel>(
   173	                        w,
   174	                        buffer,
   175	                        width,
   176	                        height,
   177	                        encoded_paints,
   178	                    );
   179	                }
   180	                _ => self.rasterize_with::<Fallback, F32Kernel>(
   181	                    Fallback::new(),
   182	                    buffer,
   183	                    width,
   184	                    height,
   185	                    encoded_paints,
   186	                ),
   187	            },
   188	        }
   189	    }
   190	}

     1	// Copyright 2025 the Vello Authors
     2	// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 OR MIT
     3	
     4	use crate::RenderMode;
     5	use crate::dispatch::Dispatcher;
     6	use crate::fine::{F32Kernel, Fine, FineKernel, U8Kernel};
     7	use crate::kurbo::{Affine, BezPath, PathSeg, Point, Stroke};
     8	use crate::peniko::{BlendMode, Fill};
     9	use crate::region::Regions;
    10	use crate::strip_generator::StripGenerator;
    11	use alloc::boxed::Box;
    12	use alloc::sync::Arc;
    13	use alloc::vec;
    14	use alloc::vec::Vec;
    15	use core::fmt::{Debug, Formatter};
    16	use crossbeam_channel::TryRecvError;
    17	use rayon::{ThreadPool, ThreadPoolBuilder};
    18	use std::cell::RefCell;
    19	use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU8, Ordering};
    20	use std::sync::{Barrier, OnceLock};
    21	use thread_local::ThreadLocal;
    22	use vello_common::coarse::{Cmd, Wide};
    23	use vello_common::encode::EncodedPaint;
    24	use vello_common::fearless_simd::{Fallback, Level, Simd};
    25	use vello_common::mask::Mask;
    26	use vello_common::paint::Paint;
    27	use vello_common::strip::Strip;
    28	
    29	// TODO: Fine-tune this parameter.
    30	const COST_THRESHOLD: f32 = 5.0;
    31	
    32	type RenderTasksSender = crossbeam_channel::Sender<Vec<RenderTask>>;
    33	type CoarseCommandSender = ordered_channel::Sender<CoarseCommand>;
    34	type CoarseCommandReceiver = ordered_channel::Receiver<CoarseCommand>;
    35	
    36	// TODO: In many cases, we pass a reference to an owned path in vello_common/vello_cpu, only
    37	// to later clone it because the multi-threaded dispatcher needs owned access to the structs.
    38	// Figure out whether there is a good way of solving this problem.
    39	pub(crate) struct MultiThreadedDispatcher {
    40	    wide: Wide,
    41	    thread_pool: ThreadPool,
    42	    task_batch: Vec<RenderTask>,
    43	    batch_cost: f32,
    44	    task_sender: Option<RenderTasksSender>,
    45	    workers: Arc<ThreadLocal<RefCell<Worker>>>,
    46	    result_receiver: Option<CoarseCommandReceiver>,
    47	    alpha_storage: Arc<OnceLockAlphaStorage>,
    48	    task_idx: usize,
    49	    num_threads: u16,
    50	    level: Level,
    51	    flushed: bool,
    52	}
    53	
    54	impl MultiThreadedDispatcher {
    55	    pub(crate) fn new(width: u16, height: u16, num_threads: u16, level: Level) -> Self {
    56	        let wide = Wide::new(width, height);
    57	        let thread_pool = ThreadPoolBuilder::new()
    58	            .num_threads(num_threads as usize)
    59	            .build()
    60	            .unwrap();
    61	        let alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(num_threads));
    62	        let workers = Arc::new(ThreadLocal::new());
    63	        let task_batch = vec![];
    64	
    65	        {
    66	            let alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
    67	            let thread_ids = Arc::new(AtomicU8::new(0));
    68	            let workers = workers.clone();
    69	
    70	            // Initialize all workers once in `new`, so that later on we can just call`.get().unwrap()`.
    71	            thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
    72	                let _ = workers.get_or(|| {
    73	                    RefCell::new(Worker::new(
    74	                        width,
    75	                        height,
    76	                        thread_ids.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst),
    77	                        alpha_storage.clone(),
    78	                        level,
    79	                    ))
    80	                });
    81	            });
    82	        }
    83	
    84	        let cmd_idx = 0;
    85	        let batch_cost = 0.0;
    86	        let flushed = false;
    87	
    88	        let mut dispatcher = Self {
    89	            wide,
    90	            thread_pool,
    91	            task_batch,
    92	            batch_cost,
    93	            task_idx: cmd_idx,
    94	            flushed,
    95	            workers,
    96	            task_sender: None,
    97	            result_receiver: None,
    98	            level,
    99	            alpha_storage,
   100	            num_threads,
   101	        };
   102	
   103	        dispatcher.init();
   104	
   105	        dispatcher
   106	    }
   107	
   108	    fn init(&mut self) {
   109	        let (render_task_sender, render_task_receiver) = crossbeam_channel::unbounded();
   110	        let (coarse_command_sender, coarse_command_receiver) = ordered_channel::unbounded();
   111	        let workers = self.workers.clone();
   112	
   113	        self.task_sender = Some(render_task_sender);
   114	        self.result_receiver = Some(coarse_command_receiver);
   115	
   116	        self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
   117	            let render_task_receiver = render_task_receiver.clone();
   118	            let mut coarse_command_sender = coarse_command_sender.clone();
   119	            let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
   120	            let mut worker = worker.borrow_mut();
   121	
   122	            while let Ok(tasks) = render_task_receiver.recv() {
   123	                worker.run_tasks(tasks, &mut coarse_command_sender);
   124	            }
   125	
   126	            // If we reach this point, it means the task_sender has been dropped by the main thread
   127	            // and no more tasks are available. So we are done, and just need to place the alphas
   128	            // of the worker thread in the hashmap. Then, we drop the `coarse_command_sender`. Once all
   129	            // worker thread have dropped `coarse_command_sender`, the main thread knows that all alphas
   130	            // have been placed, and it's safe to proceed.
   131	            worker.place_alphas();
   132	
   133	            drop(coarse_command_sender);
   134	        });
   135	    }
   136	
   137	    fn register_task(&mut self, task: RenderTask) {
   138	        self.flushed = false;
   139	
   140	        let cost = task.estimate_render_time();
   141	        self.task_batch.push(task);
   142	        self.batch_cost += cost;
   143	
   144	        if self.batch_cost > COST_THRESHOLD {
   145	            self.flush_tasks();
   146	            self.batch_cost = 0.0;
   147	        }
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    fn flush_tasks(&mut self) {
   151	        let tasks = std::mem::replace(&mut self.task_batch, Vec::with_capacity(50));
   152	        self.send_pending_tasks(tasks);
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    fn bump_task_idx(&mut self) -> usize {
   156	        let idx = self.task_idx;
   157	        self.task_idx += 1;
   158	        idx
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    fn send_pending_tasks(&mut self, tasks: Vec<RenderTask>) {
   162	        let task_sender = self.task_sender.as_mut().unwrap();
   163	        task_sender.send(tasks).unwrap();
   164	        self.run_coarse(true);
   165	    }
   166	
   167	    // Currently, we do coarse rasterization in two phases:
   168	    //
   169	    // The first phase is when we are still processing new draw commands from the client. After each
   170	    // command, we check whether there are already any generated strips, and if so we do coarse
   171	    // rasterization for them on the main thread. In this case, we want to abort in case there are
   172	    // no more path strips available to process.
   173	    //
   174	    // The second phase is when we are flushing, in which case even if the queue is empty, we only
   175	    // want to abort once all workers have closed the channel (and thus there won't be any more
   176	    // new strips that will be generated.
   177	    //
   178	    // This is why we have the `abort_empty`flag.
   179	    fn run_coarse(&mut self, abort_empty: bool) {
   180	        let result_receiver = self.result_receiver.as_mut().unwrap();
   181	
   182	        loop {
   183	            match result_receiver.try_recv() {
   184	                Ok(cmd) => match cmd {
   185	                    CoarseCommand::Render {
   186	                        thread_id,
   187	                        strips,
   188	                        fill_rule,
   189	                        paint,
   190	                    } => self.wide.generate(&strips, fill_rule, paint, thread_id),
   191	                    CoarseCommand::PushLayer {
   192	                        thread_id,
   193	                        clip_path,
   194	                        blend_mode,
   195	                        mask,
   196	                        opacity,
   197	                    } => self
   198	                        .wide
   199	                        .push_layer(clip_path, blend_mode, mask, opacity, thread_id),
   200	                    CoarseCommand::PopLayer => self.wide.pop_layer(),
   201	                },
   202	                Err(e) => match e {
   203	                    TryRecvError::Empty => {
   204	                        if abort_empty {
   205	                            return;
   206	                        }
   207	                    }
   208	                    TryRecvError::Disconnected => return,
   209	                },
   210	            }
   211	        }
   212	    }
   213	
   214	    fn rasterize_with<S: Simd, F: FineKernel<S>>(
   215	        &self,
   216	        simd: S,
   217	        buffer: &mut [u8],
   218	        width: u16,
   219	        height: u16,
   220	        encoded_paints: &[EncodedPaint],
   221	    ) {
   222	        let mut buffer = Regions::new(width, height, buffer);
   223	        let fines = ThreadLocal::new();
   224	        let wide = &self.wide;
   225	        let alpha_slots = self.alpha_storage.slots();
   226	
   227	        self.thread_pool.install(|| {
   228	            buffer.update_regions_par(|region| {
   229	                let x = region.x;
   230	                let y = region.y;
   231	
   232	                let mut fine = fines
   233	                    .get_or(|| RefCell::new(Fine::<S, F>::new(simd)))
   234	                    .borrow_mut();
   235	
   236	                let wtile = wide.get(x, y);
   237	                fine.set_coords(x, y);
   238	
   239	                fine.clear(wtile.bg);
   240	                for cmd in &wtile.cmds {
   241	                    let thread_idx = match cmd {
   242	                        Cmd::AlphaFill(a) => Some(a.thread_idx),
   243	                        Cmd::ClipStrip(a) => Some(a.thread_idx),
   244	                        _ => None,
   245	                    };
   246	
   247	                    let alphas = thread_idx
   248	                        .and_then(|i| alpha_slots[i as usize].get())
   249	                        .map(|v| v.as_slice())
   250	                        .unwrap_or(&[]);
   251	                    fine.run_cmd(cmd, alphas, encoded_paints);
   252	                }
   253	
   254	                fine.pack(region);
   255	            });
   256	        });
   257	    }
   258	}
   259	
   260	impl Dispatcher for MultiThreadedDispatcher {
   261	    fn wide(&self) -> &Wide {
   262	        &self.wide
   263	    }
   264	
   265	    fn fill_path(&mut self, path: &BezPath, fill_rule: Fill, transform: Affine, paint: Paint) {
   266	        let task_idx = self.bump_task_idx();
   267	
   268	        self.register_task(RenderTask::FillPath {
   269	            path: path.clone(),
   270	            transform,
   271	            paint,
   272	            fill_rule,
   273	            task_idx,
   274	        });
   275	    }
   276	
   277	    fn stroke_path(&mut self, path: &BezPath, stroke: &Stroke, transform: Affine, paint: Paint) {
   278	        let task_idx = self.bump_task_idx();
   279	
   280	        self.register_task(RenderTask::StrokePath {
   281	            path: path.clone(),
   282	            transform,
   283	            paint,
   284	            stroke: stroke.clone(),
   285	            task_idx,
   286	        });
   287	    }
   288	
   289	    fn push_layer(
   290	        &mut self,
   291	        clip_path: Option<&BezPath>,
   292	        fill_rule: Fill,
   293	        clip_transform: Affine,
   294	        blend_mode: BlendMode,
   295	        opacity: f32,
   296	        mask: Option<Mask>,
   297	    ) {
   298	        let task_idx = self.bump_task_idx();
   299	
   300	        self.register_task(RenderTask::PushLayer {
   301	            clip_path: clip_path.cloned().map(|c| (c, clip_transform)),
   302	            blend_mode,
   303	            opacity,
   304	            mask,
   305	            fill_rule,
   306	            task_idx,
   307	        });
   308	    }
   309	
   310	    fn pop_layer(&mut self) {
   311	        let task_idx = self.bump_task_idx();
   312	
   313	        self.register_task(RenderTask::PopLayer { task_idx });
   314	    }
   315	
   316	    fn reset(&mut self) {
   317	        self.wide.reset();
   318	        self.task_batch.clear();
   319	        self.batch_cost = 0.0;
   320	        self.task_idx = 0;
   321	        self.flushed = false;
   322	        self.task_sender = None;
   323	        self.result_receiver = None;
   324	        // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
   325	        self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));
   326	
   327	        let workers = self.workers.clone();
   328	        let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
   329	        // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
   330	        let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
   331	        let t_barrier = barrier.clone();
   332	
   333	        self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
   334	            let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
   335	            let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
   336	            borrowed.reset();
   337	            borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
   338	            t_barrier.wait();
   339	        });
   340	
   341	        barrier.wait();
   342	
   343	        self.init();
   344	    }
   345	
   346	    fn flush(&mut self) {
   347	        self.flush_tasks();
   348	        let sender = core::mem::take(&mut self.task_sender);
   349	        // Note that dropping the sender will signal to the workers that no more new paths
   350	        // can arrive.
   351	        core::mem::drop(sender);
   352	        self.run_coarse(false);
   353	
   354	        self.flushed = true;
   355	    }
   356	
   357	    fn rasterize(
   358	        &self,
   359	        buffer: &mut [u8],
   360	        render_mode: RenderMode,
   361	        width: u16,
   362	        height: u16,
   363	        encoded_paints: &[EncodedPaint],
   364	    ) {
   365	        assert!(self.flushed, "attempted to rasterize before flushing");
   366	
   367	        match render_mode {
   368	            RenderMode::OptimizeSpeed => match self.level {
   369	                #[cfg(all(feature = "std", target_arch = "aarch64"))]
   370	                Level::Neon(n) => {
   371	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::Neon, U8Kernel>(
   372	                        n,
   373	                        buffer,
   374	                        width,
   375	                        height,
   376	                        encoded_paints,
   377	                    );
   378	                }
   379	                #[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", target_feature = "simd128"))]
   380	                Level::WasmSimd128(w) => {
   381	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::WasmSimd128, U8Kernel>(
   382	                        w,
   383	                        buffer,
   384	                        width,
   385	                        height,
   386	                        encoded_paints,
   387	                    );
   388	                }
   389	                _ => self.rasterize_with::<Fallback, U8Kernel>(
   390	                    Fallback::new(),
   391	                    buffer,
   392	                    width,
   393	                    height,
   394	                    encoded_paints,
   395	                ),
   396	            },
   397	            RenderMode::OptimizeQuality => match self.level {
   398	                #[cfg(all(feature = "std", target_arch = "aarch64"))]
   399	                Level::Neon(n) => {
   400	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::Neon, F32Kernel>(
   401	                        n,
   402	                        buffer,
   403	                        width,
   404	                        height,
   405	                        encoded_paints,
   406	                    );
   407	                }
   408	                #[cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", target_feature = "simd128"))]
   409	                Level::WasmSimd128(w) => {
   410	                    self.rasterize_with::<vello_common::fearless_simd::WasmSimd128, F32Kernel>(
   411	                        w,
   412	                        buffer,
   413	                        width,
   414	                        height,
   415	                        encoded_paints,
   416	                    );
   417	                }
   418	                _ => self.rasterize_with::<Fallback, F32Kernel>(
   419	                    Fallback::new(),
   420	                    buffer,
   421	                    width,
   422	                    height,
   423	                    encoded_paints,
   424	                ),
   425	            },
   426	        }
   427	    }
   428	}
   429	
   430	#[derive(Debug)]
   431	pub(crate) struct OnceLockAlphaStorage {
   432	    slots: Vec<OnceLock<Vec<u8>>>,
   433	}
   434	
   435	impl OnceLockAlphaStorage {
   436	    pub(crate) fn new(num_threads: u16) -> Self {
   437	        Self {
   438	            slots: (0..num_threads).map(|_| OnceLock::new()).collect(),
   439	        }
   440	    }
   441	
   442	    /// Store alpha data for a specific thread (called once per thread)
   443	    pub(crate) fn store(&self, thread_id: u8, data: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), Vec<u8>> {
   444	        self.slots[thread_id as usize].set(data)
   445	    }
   446	
   447	    fn slots(&self) -> &[OnceLock<Vec<u8>>] {
   448	        &self.slots
   449	    }
   450	
   451	    // TODO: Probably worth adding a take all method and being able to reuse vec allocations somehow
   452	}
   453	
   454	impl Debug for MultiThreadedDispatcher {
   455	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> core::fmt::Result {
   456	        f.write_str("MultiThreadedDispatcher { .. }")
   457	    }
   458	}
   459	
   460	#[derive(Debug)]
   461	enum RenderTask {
   462	    FillPath {
   463	        path: BezPath,
   464	        transform: Affine,
   465	        paint: Paint,
   466	        fill_rule: Fill,
   467	        task_idx: usize,
   468	    },
   469	    StrokePath {
   470	        path: BezPath,
   471	        transform: Affine,
   472	        paint: Paint,
   473	        stroke: Stroke,
   474	        task_idx: usize,
   475	    },
   476	    PushLayer {
   477	        clip_path: Option<(BezPath, Affine)>,
   478	        blend_mode: BlendMode,
   479	        opacity: f32,
   480	        mask: Option<Mask>,
   481	        fill_rule: Fill,
   482	        task_idx: usize,
   483	    },
   484	    PopLayer {
   485	        task_idx: usize,
   486	    },
   487	}
   488	
   489	impl RenderTask {
   490	    fn estimate_render_time(&self) -> f32 {
   491	        match self {
   492	           <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

    fn flush(&mut self) {
        self.flush_tasks();
        let sender = core::mem::take(&mut self.task_sender);
        // Note that dropping the sender will signal to the workers that no more new paths
        // can arrive.
        core::mem::drop(sender);
        self.run_coarse(false);

        self.flushed = true;
    }

    fn rasterize(
        &self,
        buffer: &mut [u8],
        render_mode: RenderMode,

' --new_str '    fn flush(&mut self) {
        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            return;
        }
        self.flush_tasks();
        let sender = core::mem::take(&mut self.task_sender);
        // Note that dropping the sender will signal to the workers that no more new paths
        // can arrive.
        core::mem::drop(sender);
        self.run_coarse(false);

        self.flushed = true;
    }

    fn restart_if_needed(&mut self) {
        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            self.task_idx = 0;
            self.flushed = false;
            // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
            self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));

            let workers = self.workers.clone();
            let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
            // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
            let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
            let t_barrier = barrier.clone();

            self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
                let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
                let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
                borrowed.reset();
                borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
                t_barrier.wait();
            });

            barrier.wait();

            self.init();
        }
    }
'

        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            self.task_idx = 0;
            self.flushed = false;
            // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
            self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));

            let workers = self.workers.clone();
            let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
            // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
            let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
            let t_barrier = barrier.clone();

            self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
                let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
                let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
                borrowed.reset();
                borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
                t_barrier.wait();
            });

    fn flush(&mut self) {
        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            return;
        }
        self.flush_tasks();
        let sender = core::mem::take(&mut self.task_sender);
        // Note that dropping the sender will signal to the workers that no more new paths
        // can arrive.
        core::mem::drop(sender);
        self.run_coarse(false);

        self.flushed = true;
    }

    fn restart_if_needed(&mut self) {
        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            self.task_idx = 0;
            self.flushed = false;
            // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
            self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));

            let workers = self.workers.clone();
            let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
            // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
            let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
            let t_barrier = barrier.clone();

            self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {

        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            return;
        }
        self.flush_tasks();
        let sender = core::mem::take(&mut self.task_sender);
        // Note that dropping the sender will signal to the workers that no more new paths
        // can arrive.
        core::mem::drop(sender);
        self.run_coarse(false);

        self.flushed = true;
    }

    fn restart_if_needed(&mut self) {
        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            self.task_idx = 0;
            self.flushed = false;
            // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
            self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));

            let workers = self.workers.clone();
            let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
            // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
            let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
            let t_barrier = barrier.clone();

            self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
                let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
                let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
                borrowed.reset();
                borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
                t_barrier.wait();
            });

            barrier.wait();

            self.init();
        }
    }

        if let Some(sender) = self.task_sender.take() {

            self.run_coarse(false);

        self.task_batch.clear();

        self.register_task(RenderTask::PopLayer { task_idx });

    fn reset(&mut self) {
        if let Some(sender) = self.task_sender.take() {
            core::mem::drop(sender);
            self.run_coarse(false);
        }
        self.wide.reset();
        self.task_batch.clear();
        self.batch_cost = 0.0;
        self.task_idx = 0;
        self.flushed = false;
        self.result_receiver = None;
        // Wait for all workers to finish reset and alpha storage replacement.
        let workers = self.workers.clone();
        // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
        self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));
        let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
        // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
        let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
        let t_barrier = barrier.clone();

        self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
            let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
            let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
            borrowed.reset();
            borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
            t_barrier.wait();
        });

        barrier.wait();

        self.init();
    }

    fn flush(&mut self) {
        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
            return;
        }
        self.flush_tasks();
        let sender = core::mem::take(&mut self.task_sender);
        // Note that dropping the sender will signal to the workers that no more new paths
        // can arrive.
        core::mem::drop(sender);
        self.run_coarse(false);
        self.result_receiver = None;

        self.flushed = true;
    }

    fn rasterize(
        &self,
        buffer: &mut [u8],
        render_mode: RenderMode,
        width: u16,
        height: u16,
        encoded_paints: &[EncodedPaint],
    ) {
        assert!(self.flushed, "attempted to rasterize before flushing");

        if let Some(sender) = self.task_sender.take() {
            core::mem::drop(sender);
            self.run_coarse(false);
        }
        self.wide.reset();
        self.task_batch.clear();
        self.batch_cost = 0.0;
        self.task_idx = 0;
        self.flushed = false;
        // Wait for all workers to finish reset and alpha storage replacement.
        let workers = self.workers.clone();
        // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
        self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));
        let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
        // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
        let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
        let t_barrier = barrier.clone();

        self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
            let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
            let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
            borrowed.reset();
            borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
            t_barrier.wait();
        });

        barrier.wait();

        self.init();
    }

diff --git a/repro_dir/src/main.rs b/repro_dir/src/main.rs
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..5b4d7f88
--- /dev/null
+++ b/repro_dir/src/main.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+use vello_cpu::{RenderContext, Pixmap, kurbo::Rect, color::palette::css, RenderMode};
+
+fn main() {
+    let width = 200;
+    let height = 200;
+    let mut ctx = RenderContext::new(width, height);
+    let mut target = Pixmap::new(width, height);
+
+    println!("First fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::RED);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 100.0, 100.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+    
+    println!("Second fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::GREEN);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(50.0, 50.0, 150.0, 150.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+
+    println!("Third fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::BLUE);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(100.0, 100.0, 200.0, 200.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+    
+    println!("Rendering to pixmap...");
+    ctx.render_to_pixmap(&mut target, RenderMode::default());
+    println!("Success!");
+}

diff --git a/reproduce_issue.rs b/reproduce_issue.rs
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..5b4d7f88

+++ b/reproduce_issue.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+use vello_cpu::{RenderContext, Pixmap, kurbo::Rect, color::palette::css, RenderMode};
+
+fn main() {
+    let width = 200;
+    let height = 200;
+    let mut ctx = RenderContext::new(width, height);
+    let mut target = Pixmap::new(width, height);
+
+    println!("First fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::RED);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 100.0, 100.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+    
+    println!("Second fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::GREEN);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(50.0, 50.0, 150.0, 150.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+
+    println!("Third fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::BLUE);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(100.0, 100.0, 200.0, 200.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+    
+    println!("Rendering to pixmap...");
+    ctx.render_to_pixmap(&mut target, RenderMode::default());
+    println!("Success!");
+}

+    fn restart_if_needed(&mut self) {
+        if self.task_sender.is_none() {
+            self.task_idx = 0;
+            self.flushed = false;
+            // TODO: We want to re-use the allocations of the storage somehow.
+            self.alpha_storage = Arc::new(OnceLockAlphaStorage::new(self.num_threads));
+
+            let workers = self.workers.clone();
+            let alpha_storage = self.alpha_storage.clone();
+            // + 1 since we also wait on the main thread.
+            let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(usize::from(self.num_threads) + 1));
+            let t_barrier = barrier.clone();
+
+            self.thread_pool.spawn_broadcast(move |_| {
+                let worker = workers.get().unwrap();
+                let mut borrowed = worker.borrow_mut();
+                borrowed.reset();
+                borrowed.alpha_storage = alpha_storage.clone();
+                t_barrier.wait();
+            });
+
+            barrier.wait();
+
+            self.init();
+        }
+    }
 }

+use vello_cpu::{RenderContext, Pixmap, kurbo::Rect, color::palette::css, RenderMode};
+
+fn main() {
+    let width = 200;
+    let height = 200;
+    let mut ctx = RenderContext::new(width, height);
+    let mut target = Pixmap::new(width, height);
+
+    println!("First fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::RED);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(0.0, 0.0, 100.0, 100.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+    
+    println!("Second fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::GREEN);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(50.0, 50.0, 150.0, 150.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+
+    println!("Third fill and flush...");
+    ctx.set_paint(css::BLUE);
+    ctx.fill_rect(&Rect::new(100.0, 100.0, 200.0, 200.0));
+    ctx.flush();
+    
+    println!("Rendering to pixmap...");
+    ctx.render_to_pixmap(&mut target, RenderMode::default());
+    println!("Success!");
+}
